answer:
A Boolean formula using only ∧ and ∨ is monotone, meaning it cannot represent conditions that require a specific count like “exactly three” or “an even number,” because those involve negation implicitly. Condition I (at least three true) is monotone and can be expressed as the disjunction of all combinations of three variables. Therefore, only I is possible, so the correct answer is G.  

\boxed{G}
